scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: AI as a tutor. [pause] Guided learning, one question at a time.

scenes.name: Definition
scenes.text: A tutor is not an answer machine. [pause] It asks questions. It guides reasoning. It adapts to the student. [pause] AI can do all three — when you set it up to teach, not to answer.

scenes.name: Activate
scenes.text: Tutoring mode is not the default. [pause] You can use ChatGPT's <emphasis>Study and Learn</emphasis> mode. Or Claude's <emphasis>Learning</emphasis> style. [pause] Or just write the prompt yourself — it works in any tool.

scenes.name: Pattern
scenes.text: When tutoring works, the rhythm is familiar. [pause] Diagnose, question, adapt, scaffold. [pause] The AI provides the structure. The student does the thinking.

scenes.name: Maths
scenes.text: A Year 6 student types: do not give me the answer to 2x plus 3 equals 11. [pause] The AI asks what is preventing x from being alone. [pause] The student reasons. The AI confirms. Step by step.

scenes.name: Adaptive
scenes.text: If the student struggles, the AI simplifies. [pause] If the student is confident, it pushes harder. [pause] This is the zone of proximal development — automated and on demand.

scenes.name: Limits
scenes.text: But the digital tutor has real limits. [pause] It cannot read body language. It cannot sense frustration. It does not <emphasis>understand</emphasis>. [pause] It can be confidently wrong.

scenes.name: Teacher
scenes.text: The teacher remains the architect. [pause] Train students on prompts. Set the learning context. Monitor and evaluate sessions. [pause] The AI is the tool. You are the designer.
